methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Radian Forge is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $3M+. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector comprises 1 public comparable and 8 private peers including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity, though none qualify as narrow-stage peers for direct median-based valuation.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using the $3M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $8M. A peer-median approach cannot be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Robotics & Manufacturing universe—while 7 sector peers exist, none meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints in tightly filtered peer cohorts.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, with a median of $8M. This range reflects a 15% discount and 8% premium around the stage-progression point estimate, accommodating uncertainty in the multiplier's applicability to this specific company. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier for this subsector and the absence of direct peer benchmarking; the valuation is consequently most reliable as a reference point rather than a precise target.
